name: Fernando Pérez
tagline: IPython, Jupyter, and the open science ecosystem
description: Fernando Pérez created IPython, co-founded Project Jupyter and NumFOCUS, and has spent two decades building the open-source tools that power scientific computing. Professor of Statistics at UC Berkeley.

llmAnalysis.contextPrompt: You are analyzing a talk or podcast transcript by Fernando Pérez, creator of IPython, co-founder of Project Jupyter and NumFOCUS, and Professor of Statistics at UC Berkeley. He is a pioneer of interactive scientific computing and a passionate advocate for open source in science. He has spent over 20 years building tools that transformed how scientists work with code and data. IMPORTANT: For podcast/interview transcripts with multiple speakers, ONLY extract quotes spoken by Fernando Pérez (labeled as 'Fernando Pérez:', 'Fernando:', or 'FP:'). Never include quotes from interviewers, hosts, or other guests.

spiciness.contextPrompt: Fernando Pérez is known for thoughtful but firm opinions on open source sustainability, the tension between academic incentives and software development, reproducibility in science, and the role of community in building lasting tools. He advocates strongly that scientific software is a first-class research output, not a byproduct.
